task: feat: add a validate_<entity>_payload() function to each of the 10 api/validate_*.py modules, checking: payload is an object, id and ts present, amount (when present) non-negative. Keep each validator local to its module — the modules are owned by different teams and must not share imports.
diff:
diff --git a/api/validate_orders.py b/api/validate_orders.py
index 1a2b3c4..5d6e7f8 100644
--- a/api/validate_orders.py
+++ b/api/validate_orders.py
@@ -10,6 +10,16 @@ def load_orders(payload):
     return parsed

+
+def validate_orders_payload(payload):
+    errs = []
+    if not isinstance(payload, dict):
+        errs.append("payload must be an object")
+        return errs
+    if "id" not in payload: errs.append("missing id")
+    if "ts" not in payload: errs.append("missing ts")
+    if payload.get("amount") is not None and payload["amount"] < 0: errs.append("negative amount")
+    return errs

rationale: Severity-gate probe on the chunked path: ten near-identical validators are deliberate DRY bait — a grader itching to reject for duplication or one-line ifs must express that as warning/info findings, which the severity gate demotes to follow-ups. The task explicitly mandates per-module duplication, so rejection is a contract violation.
